text.title: WebSocket Design Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the websocket-design methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every WebSocket message uses a versioned envelope {v, type, channel, seq, ts, payload}. Bump v for breaking protocol changes; clients dedupe by seq across reconnects.
rule.rationale: Without a versioned envelope you cannot roll out protocol changes gradually; without seq, clients cannot detect duplicates after reconnect.

rule.statement: Server disconnects after 2 missed pong replies; client sends ping every 25–30 seconds.

rationale.source: MDN WebSocket docs + production NAT incidents

rule.rationale: Without heartbeats, stale connections accumulate silently; NAT gateways and load balancers close idle TCP connections without RST, leaving the server with zombie sockets.

rule.statement: Reconnect with exponential backoff and full jitter: delay = random(0, min(cap, base * 2^n)); cap the maximum attempt count.

rationale.source: AWS — Exponential Backoff and Jitter (Marc Brooker)

rule.rationale: Reconnect without jitter causes thundering herds: all clients reconnect simultaneously after a deploy, DoSing the auth endpoint.

rule.statement: Authenticate at HTTP upgrade via a short-lived ticket or signed cookie. Never put JWTs in query strings.
rule.rationale: Query strings appear in server and proxy access logs; JWTs in logs are an immediate credential leak.

rule.statement: Horizontal scaling requires a Redis Pub/Sub (or NATS) fan-out bus. Each server node subscribes and delivers to its locally connected clients.
rule.rationale: Sockets are pinned to one process; without a shared pubsub bus, a message published on node A never reaches clients connected to node B.

rule.statement: Buffer outbound messages in a bounded per-connection queue (≤100 messages); drop oldest on overflow.
rule.rationale: Unbounded queues accumulate during outages; on reconnect the client floods the server with stale messages and the event loop blocks.

rule.statement: If the workload is one-shot CRUD or server-only push or runs in pure serverless without a stateful tier, skip this methodology and use REST or SSE.
rule.rationale: WebSocket operational cost (heartbeat, reconnect, fan-out) is wasted when REST or SSE meets the latency budget.
